entries.name: Eads
entries.note: Kiowa County’s official website publishes Eads Town Council meeting information and county contacts. No separate current Town website or accessible municipal legal and permit publication was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit.

entries.name: Grover
entries.review_note: Official Weld County history confirms Grover as an incorporated Weld County town, and an official Weld County Clerk and Recorder document identifies the Town Clerk, address, telephone, and email. A similarly named municipalimpact.com website was inspected and excluded because it is for Grover, North Carolina, not Colorado. No current Colorado municipal website or online municipal source collection was confirmed.

entries.name: Hartman
entries.review_note: Official Prowers County records identify Hartman as a statutory town in Colorado. No separate current Hartman municipal website, code publisher, or town document portal was confirmed; county material is retained only as official jurisdiction and discovery evidence, not as Hartman town law.

entries.name: Iliff
entries.review_note: The official Colorado State Auditor local-government directory identifies Town of Iliff as a Logan County town, and official Logan County records identify the Town of Iliff and its local clerk. No separate current Iliff municipal website, code publisher, or town document portal was confirmed.

entries.name: Palisade
entries.note: The current official Town of Palisade site is hosted on Colorado.gov; the older CML-listed domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Paonia
entries.note: The current official Town of Paonia site is hosted on Colorado.gov; the older CML-listed domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Parachute
entries.note: The current official Town of Parachute site is parachute.gov; the older CML-listed Colorado.gov address is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Pierce
entries.note: The current official Town of Pierce site is piercecolo.gov; the older CML-listed address is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Platteville
entries.note: The current official Town of Platteville site is hosted on Colorado.gov; the older CML-listed domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Poncha Springs
entries.note: The current official Town of Poncha Springs site is ponchasprings.us; the older CML-listed domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Pritchett
entries.note: Baca County’s official page identifies Pritchett, its mayor and manager, Town address, and monthly open council meetings. No separate town website or accessible municipal legal and permit publication was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit.

entries.name: Ramah
entries.note: The official Colorado.gov site is confirmed, but automated access is currently blocked; source-category gaps remain explicitly recorded in the municipal registry.

entries.name: Rangely
entries.note: Official code, council packet, permit contact, and utility pages are confirmed; the municipal registry records the remaining archive and freshness gaps.

entries.name: New Raymer
entries.note: Official Weld County history and the Colorado local-government portal identify New Raymer as the current municipality; Raymer is retained as the historical alias.

entries.name: Red Cliff
entries.note: The official Colorado.gov site is confirmed; category-specific municipal sources remain to be discovered and are recorded with explicit gaps.

entries.name: Rico
entries.note: Official Rico homepage, board packet, planning packet, land-use amendment, drinking-water, and environmental links are confirmed.

entries.name: Ridgway
entries.note: The current official Town of Ridgway site is townofridgway.colorado.gov; the older CML-listed domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Rockvale
entries.note: Rockvale’s official identity site and Municode publication are confirmed; category-specific coverage gaps remain recorded in the registry.

entries.name: Rocky Ford
entries.note: The current official City of Rocky Ford site is cityofrockyfordco.gov; the older CML-listed Colorado.gov domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Rye
entries.note: The official Colorado.gov site is confirmed; category-specific municipal sources remain to be discovered and are recorded with explicit gaps.

entries.name: Saguache
entries.note: The current official Town of Saguache site is townofsaguache.org; the older CML-listed domain is retained as a historical directory value.

entries.name: Sanford
entries.note: The official Colorado.gov site is confirmed; category-specific municipal sources remain to be discovered and are recorded with explicit gaps.

entries.name: San Luis
entries.note: The current official site is townofsanluisco.org; its document library, permits, building-code resolution, meeting records, and Municode code publication are registered.

entries.name: Sawpit
entries.review_note: The official Colorado State Auditor directory identifies Town of Sawpit as a San Miguel County town. San Miguel County's official hazard-mitigation and emergency-management records identify Sawpit and document its unusual governance context. No separate current Sawpit municipal website, code publisher, or town document portal was confirmed.

entries.name: Sedgwick
entries.note: The official site publishes Sedgwick’s code, zoning and ordinance page, government forms, permits, and utilities application.

entries.name: Severance
entries.note: The current official Town site publishes ordinances, land-use code, zoning map, construction standards, and permit-related materials.

entries.name: Sheridan
entries.note: The official City site and Municode code provide dedicated building, fire, planning, zoning, environment, stormwater, and permit chapters.

entries.name: Silver Cliff
entries.note: The current official site publishes building/zoning, permit packets, water and sewer providers, code enforcement, and municipal-document sources.

entries.name: Silver Plume
entries.note: The official site publishes ordinances/resolutions and licenses/permits. The authorized Municode land-use code provides zoning, development permits, building-inspector procedures, floodplain, and watershed provisions; standalone municipal fire-code and broader environmental records were not confirmed.

entries.name: Silverton
entries.note: The official Town site provides building/code-enforcement and forms pages; the authorized Municode publication contains the municipal code, land-use, building, health, and utility chapters. A standalone local fire-code source was not confirmed.

entries.name: Simla
entries.note: The official site publishes building and land-use rules, permit procedures, public works, and municipal-code references. The authorized code library provides the full code; county permitting applies outside town limits.

entries.name: Snowmass Village
entries.note: The official Town site publishes planning, zoning, building code, applications, permits, environmental health, solid waste, and wildfire materials. Municode provides the municipal code and ordinance archive; Roaring Fork Fire Rescue and Snowmass Water and Sanitation District are separate partner authorities.

entries.name: South Fork
entries.note: The official Town site publishes land use, town-clerk, permit, and ordinance-index materials. The authorized Municode publication provides the municipal code, utilities, and health/sanitation chapters; a standalone local fire-code source was not confirmed.

entries.name: Springfield
entries.note: The official portal is access-restricted during automated discovery. The authorized Municode publication provides the municipal code, zoning, and health/sanitation provisions; town-specific permit, fire, and current environmental records remain explicit gaps.

entries.name: Stratton
entries.note: The official portal is confirmed as the municipal source lead but was blocked by automated access controls; no separate official code publisher or category-specific source was confirmed, so all unverified categories remain explicit gaps.

entries.name: Sugar City
entries.note: The queued sugarcitycolorado.com domain is inactive, but the current official Town of Sugar City site at townofsugarcityco.org publishes the municipal code, town meetings, contact information, and a 2025 consumer-confidence water report. Crowley County's official Colorado portal independently lists the Town Clerk and town contact details.

entries.name: Superior
entries.note: The official Town site publishes clerk records, building and zoning regulations, permits, water, wastewater, stormwater, waste, and environmental materials. Municode provides the municipal code and ordinance history.

entries.name: Swink
entries.note: The official Colorado State Auditor directory identifies Town of Swink as an Otero County town, and Otero County's official site lists Swink among its communities. The queued townofswinkco.ourlocalview.com website returned HTTP 502 during inspection and is not treated as downloadable evidence; no separate current official town code portal was confirmed.

entries.name: Timnath
entries.note: The official site publishes the ordinance archive, current land-use code, building and fire-code information, and permit services. Environmental and utility records remain an explicit continuing-retrieval area.

entries.name: Two Buttes
entries.note: Baca County’s official page identifies Two Buttes, its mayor and manager, Town address, phone, and email, and mentions historical town-law archives. No accessible current municipal legal or permit publication was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit.

entries.name: Victor
entries.note: The official City site publishes building permits, development materials, zoning applications, and land-use updates. Municode provides the municipal code, utilities, health/sanitation, and development provisions; current land-use transition materials require freshness checks.

entries.name: Vilas
entries.note: Baca County’s official page identifies Vilas as a statutory town and publishes Town Hall contact information. No separate town website or accessible municipal legal and permit publication was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit and county material is not treated as town law.

entries.name: Walden
entries.note: The official Town site publishes agendas, municipal court records, building application and fee documents, fire-management guidance, water restrictions, and public contact information. Complete code, zoning, and environmental records remain explicit gaps pending further retrieval.

entries.name: Walsenburg
entries.note: The current official City site is walsenburg.org. It publishes the ordinance book, building permits, and applications; Municode provides the municipal code, zoning, utilities, and sanitation provisions.

entries.name: Walsh
entries.note: The official portal and utilities page are confirmed. A complete municipal code, ordinance archive, zoning, building, fire, and permit publication set was not accessible and remains explicitly marked as gaps.

entries.name: Ward
entries.note: Boulder County’s official municipal-contact page confirms Ward’s town phone and email and expressly states that the County does not have Ward’s land-use or building-code jurisdiction. No separate Town website or accessible municipal legal and permit publication was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit.

entries.name: Westcliffe
entries.note: The official Town site and authorized Municode publication provide the municipal code and planning/legal sources. Custer County supplies regional planning and permit resources, which are recorded separately from Town authority.

entries.name: Wiggins
entries.note: The current official Town site is wigginsco.gov. The Town publishes clerk records, community development, permits, and building-code information; American Legal Publishing provides the municipal code and code-adoption ordinance.

entries.name: Wiley
entries.note: The current official site is townofwiley.gov and publishes notices, meetings, clerk contact, utilities, and service reporting. No accessible municipal code, zoning, building, fire, or permit publication was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit.

entries.name: Williamsburg
entries.note: The official Colorado portal confirms Town Hall, clerk, code enforcement, building inspector, water operator, and municipal court contacts. No accessible complete code, ordinance archive, zoning publication, fire-code publication, or permit library was confirmed, so those gaps remain explicit.

entries.name: Winter Park
entries.note: The official Town site publishes a continually updated Town Code, planning and Unified Development Code resources, building pages, permit forms, business-license materials, and Town Clerk records. American Legal Publishing identifies the code as current through Ordinance 645, Series of 2026; ordinances pending codification and linked documents remain subject to freshness checks.

entries.name: Wray
entries.note: The official City site publishes the municipal code through an authorized code host, ordinances and council documents, zoning and planning materials, building and floodplain applications, and a building-code ordinance adopting the 2018 International Building and Fire Codes. Environmental records remain an explicit gap pending further retrieval.

entries.name: Yampa
entries.note: The official Town site publishes the municipal code, a 2025 Land Development Code, comprehensive plan, board agendas and minutes, public works, water/sewer rates, and a consumer-confidence report. Separate fire-district operations and a complete ordinance archive remain explicit gaps.

entries.name: Yuma
entries.note: The official City portal is confirmed but was blocked by automated inspection during discovery. The authorized code publisher exposes municipal code, zoning, public services, subdivision, and license/permit titles; portal-dependent ordinance, application, and environmental records remain explicit gaps.
